skill.name: rn-performance
skill.description: Performance optimization guidance for React Native and Expo apps, focused on lists, re-renders, memoization, image loading, and profiling.
skill.summary: Optimize React Native rendering, lists, state selectors, images, and heavy computations.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, weak-crypto terms, and a comparison expression, but review found only React Native documentation and code examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Classified as Commands
low_findings.description: The cited locations are TypeScript code fences, inline Markdown code, or React Native examples. They are documentation snippets, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited external-command location is within Markdown documentation or a TypeScript example. No executable file, shell invocation, or dynamic command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain the skill description and a comment about expo-image caching. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or security-sensitive encryption code appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter text and line 271 is an image caching comment. Neither line contains MD5, SHA-1, DES, or related weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The cited line compares React component props inside a memoization example. It does not inspect hostnames, users, network interfaces, processes, or other system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is a React.memo comparison for player data. The duplicate static finding appears to be caused by the same benign comparison expression.

content.value_statement: React Native screens can lag when lists, images, and state selectors are not tuned. This skill guides targeted fixes for rendering, list virtualization, memoization, and profiling.
actual_capabilities: Recommends stable FlatList keys, getItemLayout usage, and list rendering settings.
Explains when to use FlashList for large or complex React Native lists.
Shows memoization patterns for useMemo, useCallback, and React.memo.
Guides Zustand selector optimization to reduce avoidable re-renders.
Covers expo-image caching, priorities, and placeholder usage for image-heavy screens.
Provides profiling and debugging practices for render frequency and JS thread work.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not automated profiling instrumentation.
It does not include runnable scripts, benchmarks, or project-specific measurements.
Recommendations must be validated on real devices before release.
FlashList advice requires adding and maintaining a separate dependency.

use_cases.title: Fix Slow Mobile Lists
use_cases.description: Review a list-heavy screen and apply stable keys, item layout estimates, memoized rows, and tuned render windows.

use_cases.title: Reduce Unnecessary Re-Renders
use_cases.description: Identify unstable props, broad Zustand selectors, and callback churn that cause repeated component renders.

use_cases.title: Prepare Performance Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to audit list screens, image loading, memoization, and JS thread work before release.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Slow List
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-performance skill to review this React Native list screen. Identify simple changes for FlatList keys, renderItem, and getItemLayout.

prompt_templates.title: Find Re-Render Causes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-performance skill to inspect these components for unnecessary re-renders. Focus on unstable props, callbacks, selectors, and memoization boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Tune a Large Data Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-performance skill to optimize this large React Native data screen. Compare FlatList and FlashList options and propose safe render settings.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release Performance Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-performance skill to create a release checklist for this feature. Include list virtualization, image loading, profiler checks, and JS thread risks.

output_examples.input: A FlatList screen scrolls poorly when showing many player cards.
output: Use a stable keyExtractor based on item identifiers.
Move row rendering into a memoized component.
Add getItemLayout if row height is fixed.
Profile after changes to confirm reduced frame drops.

output_examples.input: A Zustand-backed screen re-renders whenever unrelated store state changes.
output: Replace whole-store selection with specific selectors.
Use shallow comparison when selecting multiple values.
Keep object and callback props stable for memoized children.

output_examples.input: An image-heavy React Native screen feels slow during navigation.
output: Use expo-image caching for repeated images.
Assign higher priority only to visible images.
Defer heavy computation until after interactions complete.

best_practices: Measure performance before and after each optimization.
Memoize only components and computations with measurable render cost.
Validate list and image changes on representative physical devices.
anti_patterns: Using array indexes as list keys for mutable lists.
Selecting an entire Zustand store inside frequently rendered components.
Adding useMemo or useCallback everywhere without profiling evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill run performance tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance, examples, and checklists for manual optimization work.

faq.question: Can it help with Expo projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include Expo-compatible React Native patterns and expo-image guidance.

faq.question: When should I consider FlashList?
faq.answer: Consider FlashList for very large lists, complex rows, or FlatList screens that still show scroll jank.

faq.question: Does every component need React.memo?
faq.answer: No. Use React.memo for frequently rendered components with stable props and measurable render cost.

faq.question: Will these changes always improve performance?
faq.answer: No. Performance depends on device, data size, rendering cost, and runtime behavior. Measure each change.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation and examples only, with no executable commands or network behavior.
